ddls:
CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date the request was made */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the individual making the request */,
  "city_state" TEXT /* City and state of the requester */,
  "request_details" TEXT /* Details of the request */,
  "status" INTEGER /* Current status of the request (e.g., Complete, Pending) */,
  "reply" TEXT /* Reply or response to the request */,
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of request (e.g., Information Request, Document Request) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department responsible for the request */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the requester */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the requester */,
  "requester_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the request */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the request (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Target date for completing the request */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for handling the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_requester_id FOREIGN KEY ("requester_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_request_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_type_id") REFERENCES request_types ("request_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "role" INTEGER /* Role of the user (e.g., requester, admin) */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the user */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the user */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department the user belongs to (if applicable) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Complete, Pending) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated request */,
  "response_text" TEXT /* Text of the response to the request */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the response was sent */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user responsible for sending the response */,
  "is_automated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the response was sent automatically or manually */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries related to public records */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the department */,
  "fax_number" TEXT /* Fax number for the department */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL for the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated request */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the action was taken */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user performing the action */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user performing the action */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the action taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated request */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text of the notification sent to the user */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., Email, SMS) */,
  "sent_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who sent the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read by the recipient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_sent_by FOREIGN KEY ("sent_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., requester, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated request */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path of the attachment */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the attachment was uploaded */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the attachment in bytes */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the attachment (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  "is_confidential" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attachment is confidential */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attachments_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_types" (
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request type */,
  "request_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the request type (e.g., Information Request, Document Request) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the request type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_type_id")
)
